steps.title: Write the function equation
steps.content: The phrase $21$ more than $5$ times $x$ means $f(x)=5x+21$. An $x$-intercept occurs when $f(x)=0$, so solve $0=5x+21$.

steps.title: Solve for the intercept
steps.content:
Subtract $21$ from both sides and divide by $5$:
$$-21=5x$$
$$x=-\frac{21}{5}$$

steps.title: Interpret the result
steps.content: The $x$-intercept means that when $f(x)=0$, the number is $-\frac{21}{5}$. The answer is A.
